data.title: Grayscale Files for HYPE ETF Listing on NASDAQ
data.description: Grayscale has submitted a Form S-1 to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for the proposed Grayscale HYPE ETF. According to NS3.AI, the fund aims to track the price of HYPE, net of fees, and plans to list on NASDAQ under the ticker GHYP. The filing indicates that the fund may include staking rewards, contingent upon specific conditions.

data.title: North Carolina Man Pleads Guilty in AI-Driven Streaming Royalties Scheme
data.description: A North Carolina resident, Michael Smith, has admitted guilt to a federal conspiracy charge related to an AI-driven scheme that amassed over $8 million in streaming royalties. According to NS3.AI, Smith has agreed to relinquish the royalty payments and could face a prison sentence of up to five years. His sentencing is set for July 29.

data.title: Chicago Transit Authority Files Lawsuit Against Trump Administration Over Funding Freeze
data.description: The Chicago Transit Authority (CTA) has initiated legal action against the Trump administration, challenging the halt of billions in federal funds earmarked for the modernization and expansion of the city's elevated trains and subways. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the funding freeze on the city's transportation infrastructure. The lawsuit seeks to address the financial impasse that has stalled critical upgrades and expansions necessary for the city's transit system. The CTA argues that the withholding of funds jeopardizes the planned improvements and expansion projects, which are crucial for accommodating the growing number of commuters and enhancing service efficiency. The legal proceedings underscore the ongoing tensions between local authorities and the federal government regarding infrastructure funding and development priorities.

data.title: Canadian Stocks Decline Amid Gold Producer Losses and Interest Rate Concerns
data.description: Canadian stocks have lost all gains accumulated this year, driven by a significant drop in gold producer shares. Bloomberg posted on X, indicating that traders are speculating central banks might delay interest rate cuts due to inflationary pressures stemming from the Iran conflict. The downturn in the stock market reflects broader concerns about economic stability and the potential impact of geopolitical tensions on financial policies.

data.title: Global Economy Faces Long-Term Impact from Energy Price Surge Amid US-Israel Conflict
data.description: The ongoing conflict involving the United States and Israel against Iran has entered its fourth week, causing significant disruptions in global energy prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the economic repercussions of this surge are expected to persist for years. The escalation in energy costs is creating ripples across the world economy, affecting various sectors and potentially leading to long-term consequences. As the situation continues to unfold, the global market remains on edge, closely monitoring developments and their potential impact on economic stability.

data.title: Electric Capital Report Highlights Tokenization Challenges
data.description: Electric Capital has released a report identifying 501 real-world yield sources, revealing that only 34 of these have significant on-chain traction exceeding $50 million. According to NS3.AI, the report highlights that the remaining 93% of yield sources encounter seven major tokenization bottlenecks, with distribution being a primary challenge.

data.title: MEMX LLC Seeks Temporary Exemption from SEC Regulations
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) posted on X that MEMX LLC has submitted an application seeking temporary exemptive relief from specific provisions of Rule 610(c) of Regulation NMS, as amended. This request aims to address compliance challenges faced by MEMX LLC under the current regulatory framework.

The application outlines the need for temporary relief to facilitate operational adjustments and ensure continued adherence to regulatory standards. MEMX LLC's request is part of ongoing efforts to navigate the complexities of financial regulations while maintaining market integrity.

The SEC's notice invites stakeholders to review the application details under reference number S7-2026-10, providing an opportunity for public comment and engagement in the regulatory process. This development underscores the dynamic nature of financial regulation and the importance of adaptability in the evolving market landscape.

data.title: Grayscale has filed for an application to list the Grayscale HYPE ETF on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol GHYP.
data.description:
Grayscale has submitted an application to list a Grayscale HYPE ETF, an exchange-traded fund that will track Hyperliquid's native token, HYPE.

According to the S-1 filing submitted on Friday, if approved, the fund will be listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol GHYP, and will use Coinbase Custody as its custodian and CoinDesk's benchmark pricing data.

The filing also states that HYPE staking is currently prohibited, but there are "staking conditions" that may be met in the future. Furthermore, Grayscale is not the first institution to submit an application for a HYPE-related fund; 21Shares and Bitwise, among others, submitted similar applications late last year. (The Block)

data.title: U.S. President Trump Emphasizes Law and Order Amid Energy Market Turmoil
data.description: As the ongoing conflict in Iran disrupts energy markets and drives up gasoline prices, U.S. President Donald Trump is focusing on a more secure domestic agenda centered around law and order. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Trump's shift towards this redefined Republican narrative as a response to the current economic challenges. The emphasis on law and order comes as the administration seeks to address domestic concerns while navigating the complexities of international energy disruptions.

data.title: Judge Dismisses Trump Organization's Lawsuit Against Capital One
data.description: A judge has dismissed the Trump Organization's lawsuit against Capital One, which alleged the bank illegally closed its accounts in 2021 for political reasons. Bloomberg posted on X, the judge ruled that the complaint was "deficient" but allowed the possibility for the Trump Organization to refile the lawsuit if it can address the issues in its initial filing. The legal dispute centers around the closure of accounts, which the Trump Organization claims were politically motivated. The judge's decision provides the company an opportunity to amend its complaint and pursue the case further.

data.title: Google Threat Intelligence has discovered Ghostblade malware targeting iOS, which can steal private keys and read messages such as iMessage.
data.description:
Google Threat Intelligence has identified a cryptographically stealing malware called Ghostblade, affecting Apple iOS devices. Part of the DarkSword browser-based malware suite, it's used to steal private keys and other sensitive information.

Google Threat Intelligence states that Ghostblade is written in JavaScript for rapid data theft; it activates after a device is compromised, grabbing sensitive data and sending it back to a malicious server.

Researchers note that this malware does not run 24/7 on infected devices, requires no additional plugins, stops working after data extraction, and includes code to delete device crash reports to prevent Apple from receiving and flagging the malware. Ghostblade can access and forward message data from iMessage, Telegram, and WhatsApp, and can also steal SIM card information, identity information, multimedia and geolocation data, as well as access system settings.

Blockchain intelligence platform Nominis reported that losses from crypto hacking attacks fell to $49 million in February, a significant decrease from $385 million in January. The platform noted that this shift reflects a move from code-based attacks to attack vectors that exploit human error, such as crypto phishing and wallet poisoning. Phishing typically uses highly realistic fake websites and similar URLs to lure users and implant malware capable of stealing private keys and other data. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: World Leader Summit Puzzle Challenge
data.description: Bloomberg posted on X, inviting readers to solve the Alphadots puzzle of the day. The hint provided for the puzzle is 'World leader summit?' with the letters M _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ S _. Participants are encouraged to engage with the puzzle and test their problem-solving skills.

data.title: BlockSec's EVMBench Re-Test Reveals No Exploit Success
data.description: BlockSec's recent re-evaluation of EVMBench reported a 0% success rate in end-to-end exploits across 110 agent-incident pairs involving 22 real-world smart contract security incidents. According to NS3.AI, BlockSec suggested that the initial benchmark by OpenAI and Paradigm might have exaggerated AI's exploit capabilities due to the influence of scaffold design on the results. Despite this, BlockSec acknowledged the detection value of AI, noting that Claude Opus 4.6 successfully identified 13 out of 20 real-world vulnerabilities.

data.title: ThinkBig PAC mailing ads allege that New York congressional candidate Alex Bores received over $100,000 in support from the SBF political network.
data.description:
New York State Assemblyman Alex Bores, the Democratic primary candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, was attacked by mailed campaign materials from the pro-tech super PAC Think Big PAC. The materials linked him to over $100,000 in independent support from Sam Bankman-Fried's 2022 political network. CoinDesk confirmed the amount through state election filings. (CoinDesk)

A Think Big PAC spokesperson stated that Bores "received over $100,000 from Sam Bankman-Fried's political network" but denied any connection, criticizing his campaign finances in the emails as inconsistent with his voter stance. Other primary contenders include Jack Schlossberg and George Conway, vying for the seat of Assemblyman Jerry Nadler. (CoinDesk)

Think Big PAC stated it supports candidates aligned with pro-tech policies and opposes candidates perceived as unfriendly to AI innovation; Bores recently introduced legislation at the New York State level focusing on AI safety and accountability. The report also stated that Think Big PAC had previously spent hundreds of thousands of dollars on ads targeting Bores, and Bores' campaign had issued a cease and desist letter regarding the ads, accusing them of containing "false and defamatory statements." (CoinDesk)

data.title: U.S. President Trump Confident in Strait of Hormuz Reopening Without Ceasefire
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump dismissed the notion of declaring a ceasefire in Iran, expressing assurance that the Strait of Hormuz would reopen on its own. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Trump's confidence despite the hesitation of allies to provide support.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Increases Executive Compensation Following Strong Year
data.description: Goldman Sachs has raised the compensation for its senior executives, including the chief financial officer and the outgoing general counsel, following a successful year for the company's stock performance. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the financial institution's decision to reward its top management after a notable increase in share value. The move reflects the bank's strategy to retain key talent and acknowledge their contributions to its recent achievements. This adjustment in executive pay comes amid a broader trend in the financial sector, where firms are increasingly aligning compensation with performance outcomes.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Dollar Gains Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: Traders have shifted to a positive outlook on the U.S. dollar for the first time this year, driven by escalating conflict in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X that the rising energy costs resulting from the turmoil have bolstered the dollar's position as a safe haven currency. The geopolitical instability has led to increased demand for the dollar, as investors seek security amidst the uncertainty. This shift marks a significant change in sentiment, as the dollar had previously faced challenges earlier in the year. The ongoing situation in the Middle East continues to influence global financial markets, with the dollar benefiting from its perceived stability.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Comments on Oil Prices
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump expressed his views on the current state of oil prices, stating that he initially anticipated the situation to be worse than it is now. According to Jin10, Trump shared his thoughts on the matter, reflecting on the unexpected stability in oil prices despite earlier concerns.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump: Iran Strike Timeline Advanced by Weeks
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the timeline for potential military action against Iran has been moved forward by several weeks. According to Jin10, this decision comes amid escalating tensions between the United States and Iran, following recent developments in the region. The advancement in timing reflects strategic considerations by the U.S. administration as it navigates complex geopolitical dynamics. The move is expected to have significant implications for international relations and regional stability.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Founders Fund Supports AI-Driven Cow Herding Startup Valued at $2 Billion
data.description: Peter Thiel's Founders Fund is investing in a company that integrates artificial intelligence into cow herding, valuing the startup at $2 billion. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the venture's potential to revolutionize traditional farming practices through advanced technology. The investment underscores the growing interest in applying AI solutions to agriculture, aiming to enhance efficiency and productivity in livestock management. This move aligns with a broader trend of incorporating AI into various sectors, reflecting its transformative impact on industries beyond tech.
